tasks.title: Build the local viewer (`trellis serve`)
tasks.summary: A local dev server that renders the backlog from backlog.json and hot-reloads when the task files change — no deploy, no auth.

tasks.title: Viewer — dependency graph and filters
tasks.summary: Add a dependency graph and filtering by milestone, priority, and effort to the local viewer.

tasks.title: Test suite and CI for CLI and MCP server
tasks.summary: Unit and integration tests for the generator core, the init scaffolder, and the MCP tools, wired into CI.

tasks.title: Static-site deploy of the viewer
tasks.summary: Optionally publish the read-only viewer as a static site (Pages) built from backlog.json, for teams who want a shared URL.

tasks.title: Access control / SSO for the deployed viewer
tasks.summary: Gate the deployed viewer behind SSO without building auth — an access proxy (Cloudflare Access) or publishing through an already-gated host.

tasks.title: MCP server HTTP transport (port/host) + per-repo resource addressing
tasks.summary: Add a network (Streamable HTTP) transport with --port/--host and trellis://{repo}/… resource addressing, so a team can run one shared MCP server against many repos.

tasks.title: Dogfood round 2 — re-onboard Taproot with the built-in profile after the fixes
tasks.summary: After the import and scaffolding fixes ship, re-onboard ../taproot using only the published package's built-in taproot-ai-backlog profile (no hand-written mapping) and confirm the consumer path is clean end-to-end.

tasks.title: Define the Trellis backlog spec (v1)
tasks.summary: Write the canonical, repo-agnostic spec for the file-based backlog — layout, front-matter schema, vocab, and generator contract.

tasks.title: Build the config-driven generator core
tasks.summary: Generalize the backlog generator into the reusable Trellis core — validate, regenerate README + backlog.json, compute next id, support --check.

tasks.title: Build the `trellis init` scaffolder
tasks.summary: One command that onboards any repo to the Trellis backlog — scaffolds the layout, config, generated index, CI check, AGENTS section, and process playbooks, idempotently and without clobbering.

tasks.title: Build the MCP server (operations as tools)
tasks.summary: Expose the backlog operations over MCP so any MCP-aware tool can list, read, create, move, validate, and regenerate tasks in any repo.

tasks.title: Ship the process as MCP prompts + resources
tasks.summary: Carry the "work a task" and "review" loops as MCP prompts and the spec/playbooks/templates as MCP resources, so the process travels to any MCP client, not just Claude.

tasks.title: Separate the universal loop from per-repo conventions
tasks.summary: Cleanly split the portable orchestration shape from repo-specific tech rules so the same loop works in any repo by reading a named conventions contract from its AGENTS.md.

tasks.title: Dogfood — onboard an existing repo end-to-end
tasks.summary: Run `trellis init` against a second, non-trivial repo and work a real task through the loop to prove the formula travels.

tasks.title: Packaging, versioned spec, and docs
tasks.summary: Publish Trellis to public npm as `@taprootio/trellis` from GitHub CI, renaming off the never-published `ai-trellis` placeholder, then dogfood the packaged onboarding against a real repo.

tasks.title: Bake branch protection + required backlog check into the recipe
tasks.summary: Make a protected main with a required backlog-check gate part of standard Trellis onboarding — stated in the spec, documented as a forge-agnostic setup recipe, emitted by init, and pinned so a rename can't silently drop the gate.

tasks.title: Optional custom effort scales (labels, emoji, images)
tasks.summary: Let teams skin the Fibonacci effort scale with custom labels (e.g. fish sizes), emoji, and optional images — mapped 1:1 to the canonical numbers, which stay the stored contract.

tasks.title: PR title/description standard, template, and draft shortcut
tasks.summary: Enforce the shared PR title standard in CI with a config-driven, prefix-agnostic title lint; the template, draft shortcut, and MCP prompt already shipped.

tasks.title: Code review standard and review shortcut
tasks.summary: Add a repository code-review standard — a review shortcut that grounds in the repo's conventions and emits the canonical JSON findings format (file/line/severity/suggestion), scaffolded by init and graduating to an MCP prompt.

tasks.title: Work-a-task playbook (end-to-end task loop)
tasks.summary: Add the work-a-task shortcut — a fixed loop that refines a task with the user, plans with checkpoints, branches from clean main, works it end-to-end, runs the code-review playbook, fixes findings, and hands off for review.

tasks.title: Relocate the backlog root from docs/tasks/ to trellis/
tasks.summary: Move the canonical backlog root from docs/tasks/ to a top-level trellis/ folder (configurable via a new tasksDir key) so a repo's use of Trellis is obvious and stays out of docs/ site builds — a layout change (spec major bump) carried through the core, init, MCP, and the dogfooded backlog.

tasks.title: Build the backlog import engine (disparate schemas → Trellis)
tasks.summary: Build a config-driven importer that converts an existing backlog on a foreign schema into Trellis items via a declarative mapping — locating fields, remapping enums, assigning fresh canonical ids, deduping colliding source ids and rewriting dependencies, synthesizing summaries — with a dry-run default and a --check-green result.

tasks.title: Expose import over MCP and ship reusable source-mapping profiles
tasks.summary: Expose the importer as an MCP import tool and ship reusable source-mapping profiles (including Taproot's ai-backlog) so disparate repos can describe and import their own backlog schema, with an init --import on-ramp.

tasks.title: Team roster and task ownership (owner + collaborators)
tasks.summary: Add a configured team roster (active/historical members) plus optional owner and collaborators task fields, validated like the existing enums and importable via the mapping, so the backlog records who is responsible for each item.

tasks.title: Drop the README "Next task ID"; make backlog.json the canonical source
tasks.summary: Remove the redundant generated "Next task ID" section from the README and point the spec at backlog.json's nextId (plus the MCP next_id tool) as the single published source.

tasks.title: Git-derived task history and a materialized history.json
tasks.summary: Derive a lightweight per-task history (who changed it, when, why) from git and materialize it as a non-gated history.json the future viewer can read without a git runtime.

tasks.title: Importer — resolve legacy close-dates and effort faithfully
tasks.summary: Give the importer a git-date fallback for legacy close-dates and a Size→effort mapping, flagging any estimated effort so historical values stay honest.

tasks.title: trellis init — retire and reconcile a legacy backlog on adopt
tasks.summary: Let `trellis init` report stale backlog guidance for the agent to rewrite, and add an opt-in, history-preserving retirement of the imported source tree.

tasks.title: trellis init — scaffold process docs into the Trellis home, not docs/
tasks.summary: Move the scaffolded playbooks and branch-protection guide under trellis/ so onboarding never writes into a repo's published docs/ directory.

tasks.title: Importer — strip the source id prefix from imported titles
tasks.summary: When an imported item's H1 begins with its own source id, drop that leading token so titles read cleanly ("001 README Truth Pass" → "README Truth Pass").

tasks.title: Harden packaged onboarding after Taproot dogfood
tasks.summary: Address the small but release-blocking dogfood issues from Taproot onboarding: publish under `ai-trellis`, make packaged commands target-aware, order CLI output deterministically, and clarify import retirement.

tasks.title: Importer — skip generated indexes; add a general trellis profile
tasks.summary: Make import skip the generator's own artifacts (index.md / README.md) so they aren't imported as tasks, and add a general `trellis` profile that imports any Trellis-shaped backlog (YAML front-matter under active/, completed/tasks/, removed/) — so onboarding a Trellis-shaped repo needs no hand-written mapping.

tasks.title: Importer — preserve sound source ids, with a configured new-id floor
tasks.summary: Add an opt-in import mode that keeps an item's source id when it matches the target format and is unique, reassigning collisions/mismatches into the gap above the imported range, and record a configured nextIdFloor (core-honored) so freshly-created tasks begin above the imported band.

tasks.title: Importer — stop truncating synthesized summaries at a line break
tasks.summary: Fix the firstSentence summary strategy so a synthesized summary isn't cut off mid-sentence at a soft line break; end at the first real sentence terminator across wrapped lines.

tasks.title: Init — drop the placeholder example member from the scaffolded team.json
tasks.summary: The scaffolded team.json ships an active "example" member that reads like real config; make it clearly inert (empty roster with inline guidance) so onboarded repos don't mistake it for a real teammate.
